sections.paragraph:
an unused root, like the kindred roots דּוּר, תּוּר to surround (see Hartmann’s Linguist. Einleit. p. 82). Hence טוּר [טִירָה, יְטוּר].

טור

m.-

(1) a wall around about, a fence, an inclosure. Eze 46:23. (طَوْرُ a boundary, طَوَاُر a fence.)

(2) a row, as of precious stones. Exo 28:17, seq.; 39:10 , seq. [“Or of hewn stone, 1Ki 7:12 also applied to a row of other things.”]

טור

Ch. m. a mountain, a rock. Dan 2:35, 45 i.q. Heb. צוּר. Syr. ܛܘܽܪܳܐ id. [“Arab. طُورُ.”]
